experiment: Opus vs Sonnet at each pipeline stage (David 2026-06-15 overnight)
goal: Validate Opus-orchestrator + Sonnet-subagents to protect daily token budget

tailor_cover.role: Coherent EE Specialist (identical inputs to both)
tailor_cover.auditor_note: Both truthful + submission-ready, both passed ODN trap, no blocking defects. Opus sharper on JD-targeting + cover. Sonnet 'genuinely good, perfectly acceptable'. SHARED latent defect caught: Kronos '230+ students/80+ applicants/top 3 universities' not in content_library — present in BOTH (and likely prior shipped resumes) → fix repo-wide.
tailor_cover.verdict: For tailoring, Sonnet is VIABLE (36% cheaper, 19% faster, clean). Opus adds moderate polish. At sub-agent scale, Sonnet's cost/speed likely wins.

opus.result: SUBMITTED (verified already-submitted, correctly did NOT duplicate; diagnosed invisible-reCAPTCHA-v3 false-positive)

sonnet.result: STOPPED_AT_CAPTCHA (correctly) — filled ENTIRE Goalbook Lever form incl demographics, hit hCaptcha drag-puzzle on submit, stopped

submit.winner: not-apples-to-apples (Goalbook form much harder than ElevenLabs verify-only path)
submit.verdict: BOTH drove competently + honored rules. Sonnet handled the HARDER form correctly + stopped at the true hCaptcha (right call). Opus showed verify-not-duplicate discipline. No quality gap attributable to model; Sonnet fully capable of driving submissions.

OVERALL_RECOMMENDATION: Opus-orchestrator + Sonnet-subagents is VALIDATED. Sonnet sub-agents are cheaper/faster and produce truthful, invariant-clean, submission-ready work at the same correctness bar. Reserve Opus for: the orchestrator, the Master Auditor (judge consistency), and occasional highest-stakes tailoring where the moderate polish edge matters. Route all routine tailor/cover/submit sub-agents to Sonnet to protect daily token budget.
